mnemonics: HADOOP: Huge Amounts of Data On Ordinary PCs
keyPoints: Hadoop is an open-source framework for storing and processing big data across clusters of computers
Its core components are HDFS for storage and MapReduce for processing
It is designed to scale from a single server to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage
commonMistakes: Assuming Hadoop is a database (it's a framework)
Confusing HDFS with traditional file systems (it's distributed and fault-tolerant)
Thinking MapReduce is the only processing model available in the Hadoop ecosystem
